channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Study projects millions of European heat deaths as world warms
item.description: A new study finds that extreme temperatures — mostly heat — are projected to kill as many as 2.3 million people in Europe by the end of the century unless countries get better at reducing carbon pollution and adapting to hotter conditions.
item.dc:creator: Seth Borenstein, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: A Bulgarian shipping company denies its vessel sabotaged a Baltic Sea cable
item.description: SOFIA, Bulgaria (AP) — A Bulgarian shipping company on Monday denied that one of its ships had intentionally damaged an underwater fiber optic cable connecting Latvia and the Swedish island of Gotland.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Crowds of joyous Palestinians stream into northern Gaza
item.description: WADI GAZA, Gaza Strip (AP) — From early Monday morning and hours afterward, crowds of Palestinians filled Gaza’s main coastal road for kilometers as they streamed north.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Belarus' President Lukashenko extends rule after election rejected by opposition, EU
item.description: Belarus held an orchestrated election over the weekend that the opposition and the EU rejected as a farce, extending President Lukashenko's more than 30 years in power.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: What to expect after South Korean president's indictment on rebellion charges
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol has become the country's first leader to be indicted, less than two weeks after he was the first to be detained.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and FOSTER KLUG, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Auschwitz memorial holds observances on the 80th anniversary of the death camp's liberation
item.description: OSWIECIM, Poland (AP) — The 80th anniversary of the liberation of Auschwitz by Soviet troops is being marked on Monday at the site of the former death camp, a ceremony that is widely being treated as the last major observance that any notable number of survivors will be able to attend.
item.dc:creator: VANESSA GERA, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Palestinians stream back to northern Gaza after Israel lifts closure
item.description: Thousands of Palestinians are returning to the northern Gaza Strip Monday as part of the cease-fire and hostage release deal between Israel and Hamas.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: LIVE: Auschwitz memorial marks 80th anniversary of the death camp's liberation
item.description: Nazi German forces murdered some 1.1 million people at the site in southern Poland, which was under German occupation during World War II.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Holocaust survivor recalls mother's encounter with Nazi
item.description: Marianne Muller joins “NewsNation Prime” to share her family's story of courage and survival during the Holocaust.
item.dc:creator: Natasha Zouves, NewsNation

item.title: Belarus opposition and Western leaders reject election result that extends strongman's rule
item.description: Belarus’ opposition activists and Western officials have denounced an orchestrated election that extended the over three-decade rule of authoritarian President Alexander Lukashenko.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: South Korean plane crash report says bird remains were found in engines, but no cause yet revealed
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — The first report on last month’s Jeju Air crash in South Korea confirmed traces of bird strikes in the plane's engines, though officials haven’t determined the cause of the accident that killed all but two of the 181 people on board.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Fear and uncertainty after Rwanda-backed rebels claim takeover of eastern Congo's largest city
item.description: Residents in eastern Congo’s largest city of Goma are fleeing after Rwanda-backed rebels claimed to have captured the regional hub from Congolese forces.
item.dc:creator: Justin Kabumba, Chinedu Asadu, Sam Mednick And Christina Malkia, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Israel, Hamas reach deal to release hostages and allow Palestinians to return to Gaza Strip
item.description: An agreement has been reached between Israel and Hamas that involves the release of additional hostages and the return of Palestinians to the northern part of the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Belarus strongman wins a 7th term in an election the opposition calls a farce
item.description: The smiling face of President Alexander Lukashenko gazed out from campaign posters across Belarus on Sunday as the country held an orchestrated election virtually guaranteed to give the 70-year-old autocrat yet another term on top of his three decades in power.
item.dc:creator: YURAS KARMANAU, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Colombia to send presidential plane to Honduras to pick up migrants from US flights
item.description: Colombia will send its presidential plane to Honduras to pick up Colombians after the country refused to accept migrant deportation flights from the United States, causing President Trump to enact tariffs and other retaliatory measures on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Elvia Limon, The Hill

item.title: 13 UN peacekeepers, allied soldiers dead in Congo as M23 rebels make gains in key city
item.description: The Rwanda-backed rebel group M23 is battling Congolese government forces in Congo's key city of Goma, causing the international airport there to be evacuated.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Paraguay's disappeared: A dictator's shadow is a roadblock for justice, but a few keep up the fight
item.description: ASUNCIÓN, Paraguay (AP) — Despite being ousted in 1989 after a 35-year reign of terror, during which 20,000 people were tortured, executed or disappeared, some Paraguayans feel as if Gen. Alfredo Stroessner never truly left.
item.dc:creator: MARÍA TERESA HERNÁNDEZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Colombia's disappeared: As a coalition, former enemies now search for loved ones and dream of peace
item.description: CALI, Colombia (AP) — From time to time, Gustavo Arbeláez faces relatives whose losses were caused by the Revolutionary Armed Forces of Colombia (FARC), the powerful guerrilla group he was part of during Colombia’s five-decade armed conflict.
item.dc:creator: MARÍA TERESA HERNÁNDEZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: South Korean prosecutors indict impeached president who declared martial law
item.description: Impeached South Korea President Yoon Suk Yeol was indicted on charges of leading an insurrection after briefly declaring martial law last month.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: CIA believes COVID most likely originated from lab but has low confidence in its own finding
item.description: The CIA now believes the virus responsible for the COVID-19 pandemic most likely originated from a laboratory, according to an assessment released Saturday that points the finger at China even while acknowledging that the spy agency has “low confidence” in its own conclusion.
item.dc:creator: David Klepper, WFLA, NBC

item.title: UN refugee chief calls for more help for Syria as refugees begin to return en masse
item.description: DAMASCUS, Syria (AP) — The United Nations refugee chief said Saturday that some 200,000 refugees have returned to Syria from neighboring countries since the government of Bashar Assad was overthrown last month.
item.dc:creator: OMAR ALBAM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Auschwitz 80 years since liberation: Ryszard Horowitz's story of survival and making the American Dream
item.description: An exclusive interview with Auschwitz survivor and award-winning photographer Ryszard Horowitz, on his extraordinary life and his perilous childhood as a Jewish boy in Nazi-occupied Kraków.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Colombia president decrees emergency powers to restore order in coca region wracked by rebel combat
item.description: Colombia's President Gustavo Petro has issued a decree giving him emergency powers to restore order in Catatumbo, a coca-growing region wracked by a deadly turf war.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: At least 76 people killed in a hotel fire at a ski resort in Turkey
item.description: ANKARA, Turkey (AP) — A fire raged through a 12-story hotel at a popular ski resort in northwestern Turkey early Tuesday during a school holiday, killing at least 76 people — at least two of them when they jumped from the building to escape the flames, officials said.
item.dc:creator: SUZAN FRASER, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Russia warns Trump against seizing Panama Canal
item.description: Russian officials reaffirmed their commitment to the neutrality of the critical trade route and said it expects both America and Panama to continue respecting that agreement.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, NewsNation

item.title: Trump announces second withdrawal from Paris Climate Agreement
item.description: In his first term, Trump announced the pullout in 2017, with the process becoming official two years later.
item.dc:creator: Zack Budryk, NewsNation
